words.meaning: to have earned something because of actions or qualities
words.example: The volunteers deserve thanks for their hard work.

words.meaning: to be unlike or not the same
words.example: School rules differ from country to country.

words.meaning: to make someone feel unhappy because hopes were not met
words.example: Late delivery can disappoint loyal customers.

words.meaning: to decide that something is not important or true
words.example: Do not dismiss a complaint before checking it.

words.meaning: to give someone a job or use a method
words.example: The factory will employ fifty new workers.

words.meaning: to pause because you are unsure
words.example: People hesitate to ask questions in a large meeting.

words.meaning: to bring in a new person, idea, or system
words.example: The school will introduce a new reading program.

words.meaning: to move or make something move over a wider area
words.example: Misinformation can spread quickly on social media.

words.meaning: to be all around someone or something
words.example: Tall buildings surround the small park.

words.meaning: to choose in an election or meeting
words.example: Citizens vote to choose local leaders.
